name: French Caramels
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sugar, corn syrup, chocolate, salt, and 1/2 cup of cream in a heavy saucepan.
2. Cook over fairly high heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar is dissolved.
3. Reduce the heat to medium and stir occasionally until the mixture reaches the soft-ball stage (238°F).
4. Add another 1/2 cup of cream and bring the mixture back up to 238°F, stirring only a little.
5. Add the remaining 1/2 cup of cream and cook until it reaches 248°F without stirring.
6. Remove from heat and pour into a buttered 8x4 inch pan.
7. Do not scrape the sides of the saucepan as you pour to avoid sugar crystals in the candy.
8. Allow the caramel to cool completely, then turn out onto wax paper and cut into squares.
9. Leave plain or dip in chocolate coating. Make sure caramels are room temperature before dipping.
10. Wrap individually or keep in an air-tight container.
